themes.exampleQuote: Sahil provided outstanding service — so attentive and did everything perfectly.
Sahil has been very kind and helpful with accepting custom orders whenever I come with my friends.
Absolutely unprofessional, rude, arrogant and untrained staff with zero accountability.
Tried Indian food for the first time and I loved it.
Reva Modern Indian Cuisine is hands down our number one restaurant in Pittsburgh.

notable_quote: Reva first opened in June 2024 and I have been a regular customer ever since — no other Indian restaurant can match the level.
sentiment_summary: Reviewers are drawn in by specific staff warmth — Sahil is named twice — and a sense that the restaurant earns loyalty fast, but two reviews describe a sharply different front-of-house: condescending responses to basic questions and a refusal of extra cutlery, which suggests the experience is inconsistent depending on who's working.
quarter_narrative: Spring 2026 found Reva stacking reviews at a pace few East Liberty restaurants can match — 527 Google reviews with an 80 percent five-star rate just under two years after opening, with a submission as recently as today. The visual catalog holds 443 photos across a range of categories, a respectable body of work that still runs slightly below the family median. What Reva has not done is touch Instagram: zero posts, zero reels in 30 days, with the account last active 68 days ago — a stark contrast to Mola, the Pittsburgh Asian Kitchens leader this issue, whose standout signal is creator collaboration fit and whose social presence is driving the category. Holding the #1 Indian restaurant rank is real, but closing the gap to the family top requires the one signal that is clearly switched off.
tldr_read: Dominant Google review volume and a perfect conversion path, paired with a dormant Instagram silent for 68 days. Ones to Watch — #1 in Pittsburgh Indian Restaurants.
tldr_meaning: Every discoverable signal is pointed upward except Instagram, which has functionally gone dark — the review engine is running on its own momentum for now, but without social activity that momentum has no multiplier.

playbook.headline: Restart Instagram before momentum stalls
playbook.action: Account has posted nothing in 68 days and has no bio — the 37.85% engagement rate shows a primed audience waiting for content.

playbook.headline: Address the service inconsistency in public replies
playbook.action: Two reviews describe dismissive staff interactions that contradict the warmth cited in five others — a direct, non-defensive owner response on each shifts the perception.

playbook.headline: Build a creator introduction around the custom-order story
playbook.action: The flexibility Sahil offers repeat guests is an unreported differentiator — a single creator visit documenting an off-menu request is a natural fit given the 78 creator fit signal.
